title: Journalist
description: A journalist in India researches and writes news stories for publication or broadcast, conducts interviews with sources, and meets deadlines to deliver high-quality content. They work in a fast-paced environment, often under pressure to break stories and meet tight deadlines. Journalists may specialize in areas like politics, sports, entertainment, or investigative reporting, and may work for newspapers, magazines, online news sites, or television stations.
degree_name: Bachelor of Journalism and Mass Communication (BJMC)
top_private_colleges: Symbiosis Institute of Media and Communication (SIMC)
Indian Institute of Journalism and New Media (IIJNM)

career_progression.year_0_2: Junior reporter or sub-editor, learning the ropes and building a portfolio
career_progression.year_3_5: Senior reporter or feature writer, taking on more responsibility and developing a specialization
career_progression.year_6_10: Editor or senior editor, leading a team and shaping the editorial direction of a publication
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a niche online publication or podcast, or offering freelance writing and editing services
career_progression.global_pathway: Working as a foreign correspondent for an Indian publication, or joining an international news organization

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Long hours, low pay, and high stress, with a need to constantly produce high-quality content
common_misconceptions: Journalists are always in the field, reporting live
